channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: STAT+: The quest to save Grace — and clear the way for rare disease patients everywhere
item.description: How a father’s quest may shape the future of rare disease gene therapy.

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
item.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

item.title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
item.description: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."

item.title: 3br-mgd: few-shot toxicity prediction with a three-branch deep encoder and meta-learning framework
item.description: Scientific Reports - 3Br-MGD: few-shot toxicity prediction with a three-branch deep encoder and meta-learning framework

item.title: Openai secures u.s. regulatory green light for gpt-5.6 rollout, axios report says
item.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce has cleared the way for OpenAI to proceed with a broad release of its advanced GPT-5.6 model, Axios reported, citing a source familiar with the matter. OpenAI anticip… 1663

item.title: The future of governing AI agents
item.description: Autonomous security agents need governance built into the architecture across reasoning separation, continuous evaluation, progressive trust, and agent-specific telemetry at scale. ...

item.title: Healthcare interoperability apis: new marketing moments - ehealthcare solutions digital advertising network
item.description: Discover how healthcare interoperability APIs are transforming patient engagement, connected care, and healthcare marketing by creating smarter, more personalized digital experiences.

item.title: Nigeria must fund its health system, experts warns as donor support declines — expert
item.description: By Sola Ogundipe Health advocates have called on Nigeria to strengthen domestic financing for healthcare, warning that continued dependence on foreign donors could leave millions of Nigerians without access to lifesaving services if external funding continues…

item.title: Global breast cancer survival estimates in 2017–2021 to advance the WHO global breast cancer initiative
item.description: Benchmark estimates from 2017–2021 provide an updated assessment of progress toward the WHO Global Breast Cancer Initiative survival targets across all 194 Member States.

item.title: Oncology must confront hidden side effects
item.description: As a new generation of cancer drugs continues to extend the lives of people with cancer, strategies for recording and managing their toxicity and long-term effects will need innovation too.

item.title: Chromatin landscape and epigenetic heterogeneity of acute myeloid leukaemia
item.description: An ATAC-seq-based approach is used to classify acute myeloid leukaemia (AML) into 16 epigenomic subgroups, and provides insight into the role of non-genetic mechanisms in determining pathogenesis, clinical behaviour and drug sensitivity in this disease.

item.title: Diet–microbiome synergy underlies obesity-associated immunotherapy efficacy
item.description: Diet shapes obesity-associated therapeutic responses to immune checkpoint inhibitors through gut microbial metabolism and host anti-tumour immunity, demonstrated in mouse custom-diet models and human-to-mouse fecal microbiota transplantation ex…

item.title: This former deepmind exec thinks the AI arms race could end in disaster
item.description: Verity Harding tells WIRED that the US government’s nationalistic attitude toward AI is evidence that a worst-case scenario is taking shape.

item.title: AI is becoming a bargain hunter's market, with a few luxury models on top
item.description: Inference is become a commodity except for frontier models

item.title: Short- versus long-course of antimicrobial treatment in pediatric population with acute osteoarticular infections: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis compared short-course versus long-course antimicrobial treatment outcomes in pediatric patients with acute bone and joint infections. The study evaluates the clinical efficacy and safety profiles of different treatment duration strategies in this pediatric po

item.title: Smart/stimuli-responsive metal-organic frameworks for on-demand antibacterial therapy in the oral cavity
item.description: This study examines smart, stimuli-responsive metal-organic frameworks designed to deliver antibacterial agents on-demand in the oral cavity. These advanced materials respond to environmental triggers to release therapeutic compounds for treating oral infections. The research represents a novel appr

item.title: STAT+: Europe will accelerate its review of RevMed’s drug
item.description: Kailera's GLP-1 pill data raises concerns, FDA clears Vera autoimmune therapy, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a Vera kidney drug approval, a U.K. Enhertu pricing deal, and more
item.description: The FDA approved a kidney disease drug from Vera Therapeutics called Trutakna, to treat IgA nephropathy

item.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
item.description: Elon Musk's tech company released the newest version of Grok on Wednesday, promising a cheaper, more efficient alternative to other powerful AI models.

item.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
item.description: General Intuition is betting millions of hours of video game data can train the foundation models for physical AI, making it easier to build smarter robots with minimal real-world data.

item.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time&

item.title: Your gaming data could be the secret to AGI, according to this Bezos-backed startup
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at understanding how things actually move through space and time&

item.title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
item.description: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.

item.title: Mapping the path to clinical implementation of multi-omics
item.description: This Perspective maps the challenges facing clinical implementation of multi-omics, and outlines mitigation strategies involving pre-analytical, computational, regulatory and ethical frameworks, to accelerate its integration into routine healthcare.

item.title: Diving headfirst into the google newly released ‘ai governance in america’ framework
item.description: Google has released their proposed framework on AI governance in America. I unpack it. An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

item.title: Financial services’ AI dangers highlighted by regulator’s review
item.description: Britain’s financial regulator has been urged to consider regulation of large language models such as ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini because of their growing influence on consumer financial decisions. In a review commissioned by the Financial Conduct Authority and…

item.title: B2c is the new b2b; the reddit vs. geo challenge
item.description: Business Casual Is B2B marketing becoming a lot more, well, B2C? One big takeaway from Cannes Lions this year was that creators and influencers have taken over as the new celebs at the conference. Creator-style marketing has also taken over B2B tech company m…

item.title: Bispecific 10e8.4/imab broadly neutralizing antibody in people with or without hiv-1: a partially randomized phase 1 trial
item.description: A first-in-human, partially randomized and placebo-controlled phase I trial examined the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics and antiviral activity of the bispecific 10E8.4/iMab broadly neutralizing monoclonal antibody in people living with or without HIV,…

item.title: A new fda-approved oral weight loss pill requires no fasting: what makes foundayo different from other glp-1 drugs
item.description: FDA approved Foundayo (orforglipron) on April 1, 2026 — the only oral GLP-1 pill for weight loss you can take any time of day without fasting. Average weight loss: 12.4%. Here's the breakdown.

item.title: The FDA just approved the first inhaled mealtime insulin for children
item.description: FDA approved Afrezza inhaled insulin for children ages 6+ in May 2026. No needle required at mealtimes. Here's what INHALE-1 found and what families should know.

item.title: Broadcom and apple extend custom silicon pact to 2031
item.description: Probably an extension of long-running iThing innards arrangement, not a new custom AI accelerator deal

item.title: Network-based machine learning to identify biomarkers for systemic lupus erythematosus
item.description: Researchers applied network-based machine learning methods to identify biomarkers for systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). This approach integrates computational analysis with protein interaction networks to discover disease-specific molecular signatures. The study leverages collaborative expertise a

item.title: Circulating inflammatory, redox, and apoptosis-related alterations in drug-naive idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis: an exploratory case-control study
item.description: This exploratory case-control study examined circulating biomarkers related to inflammation, oxidative stress, and apoptosis in treatment-naive patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis compared to controls. The research identified specific alterations in these three pathological pathways that may

item.title: Targeted protein degradation: bridging chemical biology and clinical translation
item.description: This review examines targeted protein degradation technologies and their translation from chemical biology research into clinical applications, addressing the bridge between laboratory discovery and therapeutic development in this emerging field.

item.title: STAT+: FDA approves Vera Therapeutics kidney disease treatment
item.description: The FDA approved a new medicine from the biotech company Vera Therapeutics for patients with a type of chronic autoimmune kidney disease.

item.title: Run AI workloads on any cloud, store on Hugging Face: zero-egress storage with SkyPilot
item.description: Read the full article at the source.

item.title: Discovery of hub genes linking oxidative stress to type 2 diabetic sarcopenia using single-cell sequencing and machine learning
item.description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) and sarcopenia demonstrate a significant comorbidity, particularly in the elderly, yet the molecular mechanisms linking them, especially through oxidative stress, remain incompletely understood. This study aimed to identify oxi…

item.title: How openai plans to win over doctors, patients and hospitals
item.description: The AI juggernaut is all-in on the healthcare industry, launching three new products in the last six months. But it needs to make sure ChatGPT is accurate enough for the millions of people who already use it for health advice.

item.title: Bipartisan AI bill is getting preempted by bipartisan resistance
item.description: Efforts to enact comprehensive federal artificial intelligence (AI) regulations keep getting preempted. In the most recent example, a bipartisan discussion draft of the Great American Artificial Intelligence Act is encountering opposition from both Democrats …

item.title: Why ‘ai ethics’ is a misnomer — and why that matters for business
item.description: Wharton’s Cornelia C. Walther explains how the language around “AI ethics” can create confusion and misplace accountability.…Read More

item.title: What will AI do for us? young adults in lower-income countries feel more positive about its potential – new survey
item.description: Until now, there has been little evidence of how young people in middle- and low-income countries use and feel about AI.

item.title: Beyond automation: building accountability for AI agents
item.description: The conversation around AI agents has evolved remarkably quickly. Not long ago, most organisations were still experimenting with prototypes and controlled use cases, trying to understand where these systems might fit within existing operations. Today, that co…

item.title: How imperial college london is accelerating dementia research with a modern data platform
item.description: * Imperial College London modernized its dementia research platform to unify IoT, clinical and research data in a scalable analytics environment for researchers and clinicians. * The new architecture separated operational and analytics workloads, improved go…

item.title: Open banking moves from data rights to data pricing
item.description: Open banking has been discussed as another way to move money, where debate over who controls the data that makes those payments possible is giving way to how that data is delivered, who bears the cost of maintaining secure application programming interfaces (…

item.title: The ethics of being left out of health research
item.description: An ethics process must not become so preoccupied with the potential harm of participation that it overlooks the certain harm of exclusion.

item.title: Enforce zero data retention on amazon bedrock with bedrock projects and service control policies
item.description: With the introduction of models that require data sharing with third-party providers—such as Claude Fable 5—organizations need a way to centrally enforce data retention policies. Amazon Bedrock gives you control over whether your prompts and model outputs are…

item.title: Binoc: a field guide
item.description: Since its inception, LIL’s Public Data Project has taken on the complex problem of monitoring government data. Among the thousands of revisions to public datasets each day, most are unremarkable clerical updates: a hundred new rows here, a corrected timestamp…

item.title: The two popular statins that increase risk of erectile dysfunction according to scientists
item.description: Chinese researchers have found that two of the cholesterol-lowering drugs, in particular, may be associated with a higher long-term risk of impotence.

item.title: Nfra chief declines comment on rajesh exports' sebi submission, says probe process is systematic
item.description: National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) Chairperson Nitin Gupta on Tuesday declined to comment on claims made by Rajesh Exports regarding the submission of records to market regulator SEBI, saying the matter falls within SEBI's jurisdiction.

item.title: Meta rolls out muse, a new AI image generator | techcrunch
item.description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising, decorating and creator-based opportunities.
